scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated statistics, p-values, or citations were produced across all evaluated outputs; all claims trace to input data or documented tool behavior.

practice_boundaries.detail: Skill explicitly states recommendations must be verified by a qualified statistician before use in published research.

methodological_ground.detail: Decision tree logic, assumption checks, and power analysis formulas are methodologically correct.

code_usability.detail: Script compiles and runs; StatisticalAdvisor class is fully functional with correct decision tree and power analysis.

functional_suitability.note: Core biostatistics and data visualization functionality is complete; minor gaps in edge-case handling or output completeness exist.

reliability.note: Error handling is documented and the skill recovers gracefully from malformed inputs within biostatistics and data visualization scope.

performance_context.note: Token usage is proportional to input complexity; execution overhead is acceptable for biostatistics and data visualization tasks though room for compression exists.

agent_usability.note: Trigger description and step-level instructions are clear enough for downstream agent integration within biostatistics and data visualization workflows.

human_usability.note: When-to-Use and When-Not-to-Use sections are clearly stated; error scenarios and recovery paths are documented for typical biostatistics and data visualization use cases.

security.note: No credential exposure or injection vectors identified; input handling stays within biostatistics and data visualization boundaries.

maintainability.note: The skill is modular and self-contained, with low overhead for future updates to biostatistics and data visualization logic or output formats.

agent_specific.note: Bayesian redirect (JASP, Stan, brms) and frequentist fallback now in Limitations section; power analysis accuracy caveat added.

key_strengths: Bayesian redirect (JASP, Stan, brms) and frequentist fallback now in Limitations section — closes the v1 gap
Power analysis accuracy caveat added with G*Power/statsmodels recommendation for precise analysis
Comprehensive decision tree covering continuous, categorical, and ordinal data types
Explicit statistician verification disclaimer prevents misuse in published research
Reference files provide progressive disclosure for statistical tests, assumptions, and power analysis

recommendations.title: Power analysis uses simplified normal approximation — not fixed in script
recommendations.problem: The power calculation uses a simplified normal approximation that may be inaccurate for small samples. Caveat documented but script not updated.
recommendations.root_cause: Full power calculation requires statsmodels or scipy.stats which are not in the dependency list.
recommendations.fix: Add statsmodels as an optional dependency. Use statsmodels.stats.power.TTestIndPower for t-test power calculations when available, falling back to the normal approximation with a warning.

recommendations.title: No explicit escape hatch for survival analysis requests
recommendations.problem: Survival analysis (Kaplan-Meier, Cox regression) is not mentioned in When to Use or Limitations, creating an ambiguous scope boundary.
recommendations.root_cause: Skill focuses on standard parametric/non-parametric tests; survival analysis is a distinct domain.
recommendations.fix: Add to Limitations: 'Survival analysis (Kaplan-Meier, Cox regression) is not covered. For time-to-event data, consider a dedicated survival analysis tool.'
